name: Navajo Green Chile
recipeIngredient: 2 to 3 pickled Habanaro's (use 1/2 habanero if you're a newbie to chile-holding)
recipeInstructions:
Based on a recipe from some chile cookbook), this dish makes 6-8 servings.
1. Broil the green chiles until the skin is blistered and loose. Remove the skin while warm and discard seeds and stem. Chop into 1-inch square pieces and put in a large chili pot or crock pot.
2. Cut off fat from pork and save. Cut pork into half-inch squares and set aside.
3. Melt pork fat in a frying pan and saute onions and garlic - add to the pot. Substitute vegetable oil or skip the sauteing to minimize fat (and taste).
4. Brown pork in the same pan, adding vegetable oil if needed, then add to the pot.
5. Cut tomatoes and green peppers into large pieces and add to the pot.
6. Finely chop habaneros and add the rest of the ingredients.
7. Bring to a boil and then simmer (uncovered) as long as you can stand it (adding water or beer as necessary).
8. Simmer for 2 hours minimum to even out the heat.
9. Serve with plenty of warm flour tortillas.
Notes:
1. This recipe takes time but is worth it. Feel free to experiment and modify as desired.
2. Beware, this "chile" gets hotter after freezing. It also makes a great chile-egg burrito the next morning.
